items.q: What process moves carbon from the atmosphere into plants?
items.why: Students confuse the directions. Photosynthesis takes CO2 OUT of the atmosphere (plants build sugars); respiration RELEASES CO2. Both run simultaneously in plants, but only photosynthesis fixes new carbon.

items.correct: Water vapour released from plant leaves to the atmosphere.
items.misconception: Water flowing through soil into rivers.
items.why: Students confuse transpiration with other water movements. It's specifically water LOSS THROUGH PLANTS - significant in forested areas, contributing substantially to atmospheric water vapour over land.

items.q: What is the leading driver of global deforestation?
items.correct: Agriculture (especially cattle ranching, soy, palm oil).
items.why: Students think trees are felled for wood. While logging contributes, the largest driver is land conversion for agriculture - food production drives most tropical forest loss, especially commodities like beef and palm oil.

items.q: Which method removes CO2 directly from ambient air?
items.why: It's DAC. “BECCS” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Weathering of silicate rocks removes CO2 over what timescale?
items.why: Correct answer: Millions of years. “Days” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Permafrost holds approximately how many GtC?
items.why: The real figure is 1500. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
